name: Prosocial Readiness Bench
description: A three-part evaluation artifact for measuring observable safety refusal, dyadic cooperation, and commons restraint in LLM agents. The current pilot release includes graph-independent summary tables, validation output, and a run manifest for 13 local/open-weight model variants.
citeAs: Anonymous Authors. Prosocial Readiness Bench: Evaluating Whether LLM Agents Favor Collective Welfare Over Self-Interest. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations and Datasets submission.
conditionsOfAccess: The paper-facing release exposes derived tables, validation reports, manifests, metadata, code, and non-harmful raw evaluation outputs. Raw Part 0 harmful completions are excluded from the default anonymous supplement and should only be distributed after safety and upstream-license review.
rai:dataCollection: Rows are generated by executable experiments in experiments/part0, experiments/part1, and experiments/part2. Part 0 uses harmful-request prompts and automated compliance judgments. Part 1 uses a fixed dyadic dilemma prompt matrix. Part 2 uses repeated same-model common-resource simulations.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Part 0 compliance labels are produced by an automated judge. Part 1 cooperation labels are deterministic mappings from action tokens. Part 2 restraint labels and survival/resource outcomes are deterministic outputs of the simulation log and analysis pipeline.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Analysis scripts normalize action labels, compute Wilson intervals, aggregate model-level summaries, build cross-part correlations, and run schema and consistency validation. Harmful raw completions are not required for paper claims.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No human-subject records or personally identifying user data are collected. Model outputs may include unsafe generated content in Part 0 raw files.
rai:safetyConsiderations: Part 0 contains harmful-request evaluation prompts and may contain harmful model completions. Default release policy favors aggregate tables and filtered outputs over raw harmful text.
rai:limitations: The current release is a pilot over local/open-weight model variants. Parts 1 and 2 are single-run pilot settings; Part 2 uses homogeneous same-model societies and a simplified water-resource dynamic.

distribution.name: part1_dimension_summary.csv
distribution.description: Part 1 cooperation rates by model and dimension.

distribution.name: part2_model_summary.csv
distribution.description: Model-level commons-restraint and survival summary table.

distribution.name: cross_part_correlations.csv
distribution.description: Model-level Pearson and Spearman cross-part correlations with bootstrap intervals.

distribution.name: validation_report.json
distribution.description: Graph-independent validation report for paper-used CSV files.

distribution.name: run_manifest.jsonl
distribution.description: Manifest linking raw CSVs, metadata sidecars, validation status, and derived analysis artifacts.

recordSet.name: Model-level benchmark summaries
recordSet.description: Derived model-level tables used for paper claims across safety refusal, dyadic cooperation, and commons restraint.

field.name: primary_rate
field.description: Safety-refusal, cooperation, or restraint rate depending on table.
